title: CHAPTER 10 —
DESIGNING HUMAN APPROVAL GATES
segments.body_html:
An approval gate is a moment in the agentic workflow where execution pauses and the human decides whether to continue, r

Chapter 10 — Designing Human Approval Ga — A gate is not a plan review at the start that covers everything afterward. A plan approval authorizes a proposed sequenc

beats.text: An approval gate is a moment in the agentic workflow where execution pauses and the human decides whether to continue, revise, or stop. That definition is simple enough, but three things are often confused with it that need to be cleared away first. A gate is not a plan review at the start that covers everything afterward. A plan approval authorizes a proposed sequence. It does not authorize scope changes, tool additions, or target shifts that happen during execution. A plan is a snapshot; the work is a film.

segments.label: 01 / 10 · HOOK

title: CLASSIFYING ACTIONS BEFORE
PLACING GATES.
segments.body_html:
Not every action needs the same gate. Gate maximalism — a gate at every step — produces the forty-clicks problem. The re

Classifying Actions Before Placing Gates — The first is risk: what is the consequence if this action is wrong? Risk scales with blast radius — how many things are

beats.text: Not every action needs the same gate. Gate maximalism , a gate at every step , produces the forty-clicks problem. The researcher clicking Allow without reading is the predictable outcome of a workflow where every minor action pauses for approval. The goal is gate appropriateness: placing gates where the action's risk and irreversibility warrant a real decision.

segments.label: 03 / 10 · CLASSIFYING ACTIONS BEFORE PLA

segments.section: FIVE POINTS IN THE LOOP WHERE
title: FIVE POINTS IN THE LOOP
WHERE GATES BELONG.
segments.body_html: Tool and permission approval, before the task begins.→Plan approval, before execution begins.→Action approval, before high-risk or irreversible actions execute.→Verification, before the output is used.→External-effect gate, before the output reaches outside the system.

beats.text: The agentic loop has five phases where gates apply. They are not all equal. Some matter more than others depending on the task. All five are worth designing before the agent starts. Tool and permission approval, before the task begins. Which tools does the agent have access to for this specific task? Every enabled tool is a potential action channel. Claude Code's permissions system allows users to specify which commands and file operations run automatically versus which require explicit approval (Anthropic, Claude Code Docs).

segments.label: 04 / 10 · FIVE POINTS IN THE LOOP WHERE

title: FOUR RESPONSES,
NOT TWO.
segments.body_html:
Approval gates are decision points, and decisions have more than two options. The workflow that offers only "Allow" and

Four Responses, Not Two — The four gate responses are approve, redirect, pause, and stop.

beats.text: Approval gates are decision points, and decisions have more than two options. The workflow that offers only "Allow" and the implicit option of closing the window without clicking is a workflow designed to produce approvals. The four gate responses are approve, redirect, pause, and stop.

segments.label: 05 / 10 · FOUR RESPONSES, NOT TWO

title: A WORKED
WALKTHROUGH.
segments.body_html:
A communications director wants to reorganize three years of project folders. She asks her agent to propose a new folder

A Worked Walkthrough — Risk-reversibility classification: moving and potentially deleting files is irreversible without a backup. Some files ma

beats.text: A communications director wants to reorganize three years of project folders. She asks her agent to propose a new folder structure, identify files to archive, and flag duplicates. Risk-reversibility classification: moving and potentially deleting files is irreversible without a backup. Some files may be shared with external collaborators. Risk is moderate; reversibility is low. By the grid, this task is in the approval-gate quadrant, trending toward human-only for any deletion.

segments.label: 06 / 10 · A WORKED WALKTHROUGH

title: THE SKILL THAT
GATES REQUIRE.
segments.body_html:
Approval gates require human expertise, but the expertise is not technical. It is consequential: knowing what the action

The Skill That Gates Require — The agent may know how to click, delete, or submit. The human decides whether that action should happen, to that target,

beats.text: Approval gates require human expertise, but the expertise is not technical. It is consequential: knowing what the action would mean if it went wrong. The agent may know how to click, delete, or submit. The human decides whether that action should happen, to that target, at that moment, given what is known about what the output will be used for. That judgment requires knowing the context the agent does not have: which files are actually active, which clients are sensitive, which outputs will be shared with whom.

segments.label: 07 / 10 · THE SKILL THAT GATES REQUIRE
